ground.schema:
Node properties:
User {betweenness: FLOAT, location: STRING, followers: INTEGER, following: INTEGER, profile_image_url: STRING, screen_name: STRING, name: STRING, url: STRING, statuses: INTEGER}
Me {profile_image_url: STRING, betweenness: FLOAT, following: INTEGER, url: STRING, location: STRING, followers: INTEGER, screen_name: STRING, name: STRING}
Tweet {created_at: DATE_TIME, id: INTEGER, id_str: STRING, text: STRING, favorites: INTEGER, import_method: STRING}
Hashtag {name: STRING}
Link {url: STRING}
Source {name: STRING}
Relationship properties:
SIMILAR_TO {score: FLOAT}
The relationships:
(:User)-[:FOLLOWS]->(:User)
(:User)-[:FOLLOWS]->(:Me)
(:User)-[:POSTS]->(:Tweet)
(:User)-[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(:User)
(:User)-[:SIMILAR_TO]->(:User)
(:User)-[:SIMILAR_TO]->(:Me)
(:Me)-[:FOLLOWS]->(:User)
(:Me)-[:POSTS]->(:Tweet)
(:Me)-[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(:User)
(:Me)-[:RT_MENTIONS]->(:User)
(:Me)-[:AMPLIFIES]->(:User)
(:Tweet)-[:MENTIONS]->(:User)
(:Tweet)-[:MENTIONS]->(:Me)
(:Tweet)-[:USING]->(:Source)
(:Tweet)-[:TAGS]->(:Hashtag)
(:Tweet)-[:CONTAINS]->(:Link)
(:Tweet)-[:RETWEETS]->(:Tweet)
(:Tweet)-[:REPLY_TO]->(:Tweet)
ground.cypher:
MATCH
    p1 = (a:User {name: 'TechExplorer'})-[r1:FOLLOWS]->(b:Me {screen_name: 'TechNerd'}),
    p2 = (a)-[r2:POSTS]->(c:Tweet {text: 'New tech trends!'})-[:TAGS]->(d:Hashtag {name: 'Tech2024'}),
    p3 = (b)-[r3:SIMILAR_TO {score: >0.7}]->(e:User {name: 'DataSavvy'})
RETURN p1, p2, p3
ground.question: How do the connections between TechExplorer, TechNerd, and DataSavvy influence the engagement patterns around the hashtag #Tech2024 in their recent tweets?

cypher:
MATCH (u1:User {name: 'TechExplorer'})-[:POSTS]->(t1:Tweet)-[:TAGS]->(h:Hashtag {name: 'Tech2024'}),       (u2:User {name: 'TechNerd'})-[:POSTS]->(t2:Tweet)-[:TAGS]->(h),       (u3:User {name: 'DataSavvy'})-[:POSTS]->(t3:Tweet)-[:TAGS]->(h)
WITH u1, u2, u3, t1, t2, t3
MATCH (u1)-[:INTERACTS_WITH*]->(u2), (u1)-[:INTERACTS_WITH*]->(u3),       (u2)-[:INTERACTS_WITH*]->(u3)
RETURN t1, t2, t3
